nodes.name: LoomCycle MCP Server: Slack

parameters.toolDescription: Send a message to Slack. Pass the message body as the prompt; a loomcycle agent with access to the Slack MCP server handles the posting.

nodes.notes: On the first workflow run, the LoomCycleMcpServerTool registers `slack-mcp` in the loomcycle substrate via MCPServerDef.create. Subsequent runs are no-ops (idempotent ensure: get → on NotFoundError, create). The token substitutes from the loomcycle deployment's `LOOMCYCLE_SLACK_TOKEN` env var at request time — plaintext credentials never traverse this wire path. Operators MUST mirror the env var on the loomcycle side.

options.systemMessage: You are a Slack-aware assistant. When the user asks to send a message to Slack, use the post_to_slack tool with the message body as the prompt.

meta.description: The crown-jewel workflow: drag an MCP server into n8n's AI Agent canvas, and it auto-provisions in the loomcycle substrate via MCPServerDef. The AI Agent calls `post_to_slack` → a one-shot loomcycle agent spawns with `allowed_tools: ['mcp__slack-mcp__*']` → the agent posts to Slack via the registered MCP server. Required loomcycle version: >= 0.9.2 (PR #177 substrate-side MCPServerDef). Required env var on loomcycle: `LOOMCYCLE_SLACK_TOKEN`.
